mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of rate increase months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in a single month
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >55% of triggered months, or the signal fails to separate bull/bear outcomes with a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth, which sustains corporate earnings expectations and drives positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both positive month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day returns in >60% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above 1.5% annually during a late-cycle expansion indicates capacity constraints and rising input costs, which compress profit margins and generate negative monthly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Signal produces positive 20-day returns in >70% of late-cycle observations, or regime classification fails to improve predictive accuracy over a baseline model.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly correlates with stronger corporate revenue forecasts, producing positive short-term S&P 500 momentum as earnings revisions propagate through equity pricing models.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of high-GDP quarters, or the series shows zero predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation exceeding 2.5% forces the Federal Reserve to accelerate tightening, which raises the cost of capital and suppresses equity valuations over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of threshold-crossing months, or inflation spikes fail to precede equity drawdowns with a lag of less than 30 days.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which boosts consumer spending confidence and drives positive monthly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Joint labor strength signals yield negative 20-day returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from independent proxy effects in regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden contraction in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals supply chain fragility, triggering immediate risk aversion and negative weekly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of contraction months within the specified regime, or the signal loses statistical significance when controlling for monetary policy shifts.
